title: [Serperior] Battle Guide
summary: A paradoxical special setup sweeper that turns [leaf-storm] into a +2 SpA boost every c[lick] thanks to [contrary], and rides 113 Speed to close games.
weaknesses: Narrow cove[rage] pool is walled by [Steel] and [Poison] pivots
Priority attackers shut the plan down before setup lands

recommendedMoves.reason: Primary STAB that raises SpA by 2 via [contrary]

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Serperior] is a mono-[Grass] special sweeper whose entire competitive identity comes from the [contrary] ability. 75 Special Attack looks ordinary, but because [contrary] inverts stat drops, every click of [leaf-storm] raises SpA by two stages instead of lowering it. After two attack turns, [Serperior] is already at +4 and usually winning the damage race against every neutral wall. Base 113 Speed lets it outrun most unboosted attackers, and 95/95 mixed bulk expands its survivable matchup list significantly.

## Strengths
The [contrary] + [leaf-storm] core is one of the strongest setup loops in the meta because no dedicated setup turn is required. Every attack is both damage and a boost. [giga-drain] provides passive recovery once the sweep starts rolling, [dragon-pulse] covers [Dragon] and mirror [Grass] matchups that [leaf-storm] cannot punish, and [glare] is the utility card that steals turns from faster attackers via paralysis. Because the setup is the attack itself, opponents cannot easily punish the 'wasted' setup turn.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Five 2x weaknesses — [Ice], [Fire], [Poison], [Bug], and [Flying] — make [Serperior] a liability against offense. Its coverage pool is narrow, so [Steel], [Fire], and [Poison] pivots wall it cleanly and force hard switches. Priority moves and choice-scarf users also threaten to remove [Serperior] before the second boosted [leaf-storm] lands. If the first two turns aren't safe, the sweep never starts. Tera [Fire] is the canonical flip that patches the worst weakness spread in one move.

## Recommended Moveset
[leaf-storm] is the locked-in primary STAB because it's both the damage and the setup. After two clicks [Serperior] sits at +4 SpA, at which point [giga-drain] becomes the safe cleanup option that recovers HP on impact. [dragon-pulse] handles [Dragon] and mirror [Grass] pivots that resist the STAB combo. The last slot is [glare], whose paralysis chance turns fast [revenge] killers into free [Serperior] turns. [taunt] is a reasonable alternative if the team already has enough status support.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default insurance against priority so the first [leaf-storm] always fires. [leftovers] is the alternate item for long-game teams that rely on [giga-drain] recovery loops. [miracle-seed] boosts [Grass] STAB without locking the move, which helps when early damage breakpoints are critical. The ability must always be [contrary] — no other option exists competitively. Tera [Fire] flips the worst defensive weaknesses, while Tera [Grass] maxes raw STAB output for a pure offensive ceiling.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Serperior] appreciates a [Steel] or [Poison] remover teammate — [Ground] or [Fire] attackers that can soften the walls [Serperior] cannot break. Priority counters like Intimidate users or [magic-guard] partners reduce the early-game risk, and a fast cleaner behind [Serperior] takes advantage of the [glare] paralysis turns by starting its own setup. A lead hazard remover is also valuable because [Serperior] hates entering into chip damage.

## Play Patterns
Open the first [leaf-storm] into a physical wall to maximize the boost curve, because [Serperior]'s special bulk comfortably absorbs neutral physical hits. Once at +4, transition to [giga-drain] for the HP recovery loop, and save [dragon-pulse] for the exact moment a [Grass]-resistant pivot appears. Deploy the Tera trigger the moment a 2x weakness move is telegraphed — that single swap is typically what carries the sweep to the end of the game.
